columns.extraction_plan: The add-on treatment is Docetaxel. This is explicitly stated in the title of the paper on page 1: "Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone". It is also mentioned in the abstract on page 1: "Survival benefits of docetaxel (D) added to androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT)". Further details are in the "Patients and methods" section on page 2, stating "Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone."

columns.extraction_plan: This column refers to the overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence in randomized controlled trials (RCTs), which is typically performed as part of a systematic review or meta-analysis to assess the quality or risk of bias of included studies. This document is a primary research article reporting the results of an RCT and does not contain such an external assessment or a self-assessment using a standardized risk judgment framework for its own evidence criteria. While the paper discusses its limitations (e.g., lack of statistical power, retrospective analysis), these are not presented as an "overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE)".

plans.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

columns.column_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1
columns.extraction_plan: The class of agent for Docetaxel (D), which is part of Treatment Arm 1 (ADT plus D), is chemotherapy. This is explicitly stated and implied throughout the text. For example, on page 1, in the abstract, it mentions 'Survival benefits of docetaxel (D) added to androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) were shown in the CHAARTED trial in patients with metastatic high-volume disease (HVD).' and 'The role of chemotherapy in metastatic non castrate prostate cancer (mNCPC) is debated.' On page 2, in the 'Patient summary', it states 'early chemotherapy should be discussed with all patients...'.
columns.column_name_raw: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

columns.extraction_plan: The total number of participants in the control arm (ADT arm) is 193. This information is reported in the text on page 2, in the "Patients and methods" section: "From October 2004 to December 2008, 385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm." It is also confirmed in Table 1 on page 3, under the column "ADT (n = 193)".

The control arm is "ADT alone" (or simply "ADT"). This is explicitly stated in the abstract on page 1: "Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone". It is also referred to as "ADT" in Table 1 on page 3 and Table 2 on page 4, and in the "Patients and methods" section on page 2: "Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone."

The number of patients randomized to the ADT plus D arm was 192 (page 2, text). The text also states that "patients received a median number of eight and six cycles of D" (page 5, text), implying actual administration. However, an explicit count and percentage of participants in the treatment arm who *received* docetaxel (e.g., "X patients (Y%) received docetaxel") is not reported.

In the control arm (ADT arm), 152 patients (79%) received docetaxel as salvage therapy. Locate the paragraph on page 5 that states: "In the GETUG-AFU15 study, in contrast, the vast majority of patients (79%) in the ADT arm received so-called salvage D within a median time of 18.5 mo (95% CI, 2.9-179) indicating that this trial was more a trial of early versus delayed D." The number of patients in the ADT arm is 193 (page 2, text). Calculate 79% of 193 to get the count (0.79 * 193 = 152.47, rounded to 152).
Extract the value from Table 1, row "Gleason score, n (%)", sub-row "<7", column "ADT plus D (n = 192)". The value is 84 (45%).
Extract the value from Table 1, row "Gleason score, n (%)", sub-row "<7", column "ADT (n = 193)". The value is 78 (41%).
Extract the value from Table 1, row "Gleason score, n (%)", sub-row ">8", column "ADT plus D (n = 192)". The value is 103 (55%).
Extract the value from Table 1, row "Gleason score, n (%)", sub-row ">8", column "ADT (n = 193)". The value is 113 (59%).
The NCT identifier is reported on page 2, in the "Patients and methods" section, under the sentence "The trial is registered with ClinicalTrials.gov (NCT00104715)."
The PubMed ID is not reported in the PDF. A DOI is provided, but not a PubMed ID.
The trial name is explicitly stated in the title of the article on page 1.
The author list is provided on page 1, at the beginning of the article.
The publication year is indicated at the top of page 1 as "EUROPEAN UROLOGY 70 (2016)256-262".
The document is a multi-page article with detailed methods, results, discussion, and references, indicating it is a full publication.

The phase is mentioned in the title of the article on page 1 as "Randomized Phase 3 GETUG-AFU15 Trial". It is also stated in the "Design, setting, and participants" section of the abstract on page 1 as "Randomized phase 3 trial".

The title of the article on page 1 includes "Long-term Survival Analysis". The abstract on page 1 states the objective is "to update overall survival (OS) data of the GETUG-AFU15 study." The introduction on page 2 also mentions "updated survival analyses. We present long-term outcomes...".
The median age for the treatment arm (ADT plus D) is reported as 63.0 years in Table 1, under the row "Age, yr, median (IQR)" and column "ADT plus D (n = 192)".
The median age for the control arm (ADT) is reported as 64.0 years in Table 1, under the row "Age, yr, median (IQR)" and column "ADT (n = 193)".
Extract the value "83.9" from the "Median follow-up, mo" row under the "GETUG-AFU 15" column in Table 2.
The median OS for the overall population in the ADT plus D arm is reported in Table 2 on page 4, under the "GETUG-AFU 15" section, "All patients" column, row "ADT plus D, mo". The value is 62.1.
The median OS for the overall population in the ADT arm is reported in Table 2 on page 4, under the "GETUG-AFU 15" section, "All patients" column, row "ADT, mo". The value is 48.6.
The median OS for high-volume disease (HVD) patients in the ADT plus D arm is reported in Table 2 on page 4, under the "GETUG-AFU 15" section, "HVD" column, row "ADT plus D, mo". The value is 39.8.
The median OS for high-volume disease (HVD) patients in the ADT arm is reported in Table 2 on page 4, under the "GETUG-AFU 15" section, "HVD" column, row "ADT, mo". The value is 35.1.

The median OS for low-volume disease (LVD) patients in the ADT plus D arm is reported in Table 2 on page 4, under the "GETUG-AFU 15" section, "LVD" column, row "ADT plus D, mo". The value is NR (Not Reached).
The median OS for low-volume disease (LVD) patients in the ADT arm is reported in Table 2 on page 4, under the "GETUG-AFU 15" section, "LVD" column, row "ADT, mo". The value is 83.4.

The median number of docetaxel (D) cycles for patients in the GETUG-AFU15 trial's ADT plus D arm was 8 cycles. Docetaxel was administered in 21-day cycles. Calculate the total duration: 8 cycles * 21 days/cycle = 168 days. Convert days to months (assuming 30.4375 days/month for an average month): 168 days / 30.4375 days/month ≈ 5.5 months.

The control arm received Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) alone, which is described as continuous therapy. The document does not report a specific median finite duration in months for this continuous on-treatment therapy. While median overall survival (48.6 months) and biochemical progression-free survival (12.9 months) for the ADT arm are reported in Table 2 on page 4, these are not explicitly stated as 'median on-treatment duration' for the continuous ADT regimen.

The median biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) for the overall population in the ADT plus D arm is reported as 22.9 months in the "Biochemical and radiographic progression-free survival" subsection of the Results on page 4, first paragraph.

The median biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) for the overall population in the ADT arm is reported as 12.9 months in the "Biochemical and radiographic progression-free survival" subsection of the Results on page 4, first paragraph.

The median biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) for the HVD group in the ADT plus D arm is reported as 15.2 months in the "Biochemical and radiographic progression-free survival" subsection of the Results on page 4, first paragraph.

The median biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) for the HVD group in the ADT arm is reported as 9.2 months in the "Biochemical and radiographic progression-free survival" subsection of the Results on page 4, first paragraph.

The median biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) for the LVD group in the ADT plus D arm is reported as 40.9 months in the "Biochemical and radiographic progression-free survival" subsection of the Results on page 4, first paragraph.

The median biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) for the LVD group in the ADT arm is reported as 22.4 months in the "Biochemical and radiographic progression-free survival" subsection of the Results on page 4, first paragraph.

The number of deaths in the treatment arm (ADT plus D) is reported in the text on page 3, within Chunk 4. The text states: "After a median follow-up of 83.9 mo (95% CI, 82.9-84.7), 242 patients had died (127 in the ADT arm and 115 in the ADT plus D arm; 147 and 95 in the HVD and LVD subgroups, respectively)." The value for the ADT plus D arm is 115.

The number of deaths in the control arm (ADT) is reported in the text on page 3, within Chunk 4. The text states: "After a median follow-up of 83.9 mo (95% CI, 82.9-84.7), 242 patients had died (127 in the ADT arm and 115 in the ADT plus D arm; 147 and 95 in the HVD and LVD subgroups, respectively)." The value for the ADT arm is 127.

The number of arms included is 2. This is reported in the text on page 1, in the abstract under "Design, setting, and participants", stating "Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone". It is also explicitly stated in the "Patients and methods" section on page 2: "The GETUG-AFU15 trial is a French multicenter open-label randomized study [13]. From October 2004 to December 2008, 385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm."

Kaplan-Meier curves are provided, but a precise percentage is not explicitly stated.

The document reports median biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) and radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS) in months for the ADT plus D (treatment) arm, but not a percentage rate (e.g., 1-year PFS rate). Specifically, median bPFS was 22.9 mo (95% CI, 19.5–28.4) and median rPFS was 22.9 mo (95% CI, 20.5-31.4) for the ADT plus D arm. The hazard ratio for bPFS (ADT plus D vs ADT) was 0.67 (95% CI, 0.54–0.84) and for rPFS (ADT plus D vs ADT) was 0.69 (95% CI, 0.55–0.87). However, a direct PFS rate as a percentage is not reported.

The document reports median biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) and radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS) in months for the ADT (control) arm, but not a percentage rate (e.g., 1-year PFS rate). Specifically, median bPFS was 12.9 mo (95% CI, 11.9–17.7) and median rPFS was 15.3 mo (95% CI, 12.4-19.8) for the ADT arm. The hazard ratio for bPFS (ADT plus D vs ADT) was 0.67 (95% CI, 0.54–0.84) and for rPFS (ADT plus D vs ADT) was 0.69 (95% CI, 0.55–0.87). However, a direct PFS rate as a percentage is not reported.

The primary endpoint is reported in the "Abstract" section on page 1 and in the "2.1. Statistics" section on page 2. It is stated as "OS" (Overall Survival).

The "Quality of Life Scale" is not reported as an outcome for the GETUG-AFU15 trial in this PDF. The primary and secondary endpoints for the GETUG-AFU15 trial are listed on page 2 under "Outcome measurements and statistical analysis" and include overall survival (OS), biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS), and radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS), but not quality of life. While "quality of life" is mentioned on page 6 (References, [26] and [27]) in the context of a *different* trial (GETUG 12), it is not an outcome reported for the GETUG-AFU15 study.

The clinical trial (GETUG-AFU15) does not report results on the quality of life of participants in this PDF. While "quality of life" is mentioned in the titles of references [26] and [27] on page 6, these refer to the GETUG 12 trial, not the GETUG-AFU15 trial which is the subject of this paper. The main text, abstract, and results sections of the paper do not include any data or discussion regarding quality of life for the GETUG-AFU15 study.

The PDF states, "The GETUG-AFU15 trial is a French multicenter open-label randomized study [13]. From October 2004 to December 2008, 385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm." (Chunk 4, page 2). Since it's a French multicenter study, all participants are from Europe. The treatment arm is "ADT plus D". Therefore, the value is 192 (100%).

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Synchronous
columns.extraction_plan: The results are reported for patients with synchronous metastases. On page 3, section 3.1, under "Overall survival", the text states: "Patients with metastatic disease after failure of local treatment (28%) had a significantly longer median OS than those with metastases at diagnosis (72%) at 83.1 mo (95% CI, 68.6-NR) versus 46.5 mo (95% CI, 40.5-54.6)". This indicates that "metastases at diagnosis" (synchronous) is a reported prognostic group. Further confirmation is on page 5, section 4, which states: "But no significant difference in OS was observed between treatment arms (ADT plus D vs ADT) between patients with de novo and secondary metastatic diseases." "De novo" refers to synchronous.
columns.column_name_raw: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Synchronous

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Metachronous
columns.extraction_plan: The results are reported for patients with metachronous metastases. On page 3, section 3.1, under "Overall survival", the text states: "Patients with metastatic disease after failure of local treatment (28%) had a significantly longer median OS than those with metastases at diagnosis (72%) at 83.1 mo (95% CI, 68.6-NR) versus 46.5 mo (95% CI, 40.5-54.6)". "Metastatic disease after failure of local treatment" refers to metachronous metastases. Further confirmation is on page 5, section 4, which states: "But no significant difference in OS was observed between treatment arms (ADT plus D vs ADT) between patients with de novo and secondary metastatic diseases." "Secondary metastatic diseases" refers to metachronous.
columns.column_name_raw: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Metachronous

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | High volume
columns.extraction_plan: The results are reported for patients with high volume metastases. On page 2, section 1, the text states: "We retrospectively retrieved data on metastatic volume from medical files of all patients included in the GETUG-AFU15 study, applying the CHAARTED definition of HVD and low-volume disease (LVD) and updated survival analyses. We present long-term outcomes in the overall population and in the HVD and LVD subgroups." HVD stands for high-volume disease. On page 3, section 3.1, the text explicitly states: "In patients with HVD, median OS was 4.7 mo longer in the ADT plus D arm...". Table 2 on page 4 also presents survival data under the "HVD" column.
columns.column_name_raw: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | High volume

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Low volume
columns.extraction_plan: The results are reported for patients with low volume metastases. On page 2, section 1, the text states: "We retrospectively retrieved data on metastatic volume from medical files of all patients included in the GETUG-AFU15 study, applying the CHAARTED definition of HVD and low-volume disease (LVD) and updated survival analyses. We present long-term outcomes in the overall population and in the HVD and LVD subgroups." LVD stands for low-volume disease. On page 3, section 3.1, the text explicitly states: "In patients with LVD, median OS was not reached (NR; 95% CI, 69.5-NR) in the ADT plus D arm...". Table 2 on page 4 also presents survival data under the "LVD" column.
columns.column_name_raw: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Low volume

columns.extraction_plan: The "Article info" section in the abstract on page 1 states: "Secondary end points were biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) and radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS)."

The value is reported in the text on page 4, under section "3.2. Biochemical and radiographic progression-free survival". It states: "The bPFS was significantly longer for patients randomized in the ADT plus D arm in the overall population at 22.9 mo (95% CI, 19.5-28.4)". The "ADT plus D arm" is the treatment arm. This is also confirmed in Figure 4 on page 4, where "ADT plus D: 22.9 (19.5-28.4)" is listed.

The value is reported in the text on page 4, under section "3.2. Biochemical and radiographic progression-free survival". It states: "versus 12.9 mo (95% CI, 11.9–17.7) (HR: 0.67 [95% CI, 0.54–0.84]; p < 0.001)". This refers to the "ADT arm", which is the control arm. This is also confirmed in Figure 4 on page 4, where "ADT: 12,9 (11,9-17,7)" is listed.

The total number of participants is reported as 385. This can be extracted from the "Article info" section on page 1, which states "Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients". It is also confirmed in the "Patients and methods" section on page 2: "From October 2004 to December 2008, 385 patients were enrolled". Additionally, Table 1 on page 3 is titled "Characteristics of the patients (n = 385)", and Table 2 on page 4 has a column "All patients n = 385".

The treatment arms are "ADT plus D" (or "ADT plus Docetaxel") and "ADT alone". This information is explicitly stated in the title on page 1, in the abstract on page 1, and in the "Patients and methods" section on page 2. For example, on page 1, in the abstract under "Design, setting, and participants", it states: "Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients".

The total number of participants in the experimental arm (ADT plus D) is reported as 192. This can be extracted from Table 1 on page 3, under the column "ADT plus D (n = 192)". It is also mentioned in the text in Section 3. Results on page 3: "The GETUG-AFU15 trial randomized 385 patients including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm."
The value is reported in the text. Extract the regimen details for the "ADT plus D" arm from the "Patients and methods" section.

The treatment classes involved in the study are Androgen Deprivation Therapy and Chemotherapy. This is evident from the abstract and introduction, which refer to "Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT)" and "docetaxel (D)", with docetaxel being a chemotherapy agent. For example, the abstract states: "Survival benefits of docetaxel (D) added to androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT)".

The name of the clinical study is "GETUG-AFU15 Trial". It is reported in the title of the paper on page 1 (TEXT) and frequently throughout the text, for example, in the "Introduction" section on page 2 (TEXT) and in Table 2 on page 4 (TABLE).

Locate the "Patients and methods" section. In the first paragraph, find the sentence: "Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone." and the subsequent sentence: "ADT consisted of orchiectomy or luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone agonists, alone or combined with nonsteroidal androgen receptor inhibitors."

columns.column_name: Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: The number and percentage of participants in the treatment arm (ADT plus D) with high-volume disease is reported in Table 1, on page 3. Extract the value from the row "High-volume disease (%)" and column "ADT plus D (n = 192)".

columns.column_name: Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Control
columns.extraction_plan: The number and percentage of participants in the control arm (ADT) with high-volume disease is reported in Table 1, on page 3. Extract the value from the row "High-volume disease (%)" and column "ADT (n = 193)".

columns.column_name: Volume of disease - N (%) | Low | Control
columns.extraction_plan: The number and percentage of participants in the control arm (ADT) with low-volume disease is reported in Table 1, on page 3. Extract the value from the row "Low-volume disease (%)" and column "ADT (n = 193)".
